headline: Student Debt Exceeds RM15,000 Average Before First Job
context: Combined PTPTN loans, credit card debt, and personal loans leave average fresh graduates with RM15,000 in debt.

cards.big: Average fresh graduate carries RM15,000 in combined debt before earning first salary
cards.sub: PTPTN accounts for RM12,000 with remaining from credit and personal loans

cards.big: Graduate debt-to-starting-salary ratio in Malaysia is 5.4 months versus 2.1 in Singapore
cards.sub: Higher debt burden relative to earnings delays financial independence

cards.big: AKPK reported 28% of individuals seeking debt counseling in 2025 were under 30
cards.sub: Early-career financial distress has long-term implications for wealth building

cards.big: Denmark provides free university education plus monthly stipend of RM3,800 equivalent
cards.sub: Zero-debt graduation model enables faster economic participation of young people

cards.big: Saddling young people with debt before their careers begin is an intergenerational tax on ambition
Should Malaysia move toward free public university education funded by progressive taxation
